title: OpenClaw Just Got a Physical Body — DeepMirror Put It Inside a Robot
author: OpenClawNewsFactor
summary_bullets: Open Claw started as a messaging agent on your phone.
On March 5th, Deep Mirror put it inside a robot and now it can walk, see, reason, and act in the physical world without a human in the loop.
Hong Kong AI robotics company Deep Mirror just announced full integration of Open Claw into their physical AI stack built on Uni Tree Robotics hardware.
This is the first official deployment of OpenClaw as the reasoning brain of an autonomous physical agent and it signals that OpenClaw's architecture is no longer just about digital workflows.
Here is the problem Deep Mirror was solving.
Large language models are brilliant at complex planning.
Give them a goal, describe the environment, and they will generate a detailed step-by-step strategy.

But the moment that strategy needs to execute in the real physical world, reading sensor data, adjusting to unexpected obstacles, making split-second control decisions within hardware safety constraints, LLMs fall apart.
The reasoning layer and the action layer have always been disconnected.
Deep mirrors integration uses openclaw as the bridge between those two layers.
